title: Four Songs Of Four Seasons by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
I. WINTER IN NORTHUMBERLAND
OUTSIDE the garden
The wet skies harden;
The gates are barred on
The summer side:
"Shut out the flower-time,
Sunbeam and shower-time;
Make way for our time,"
meaning: "Four Songs Of Four Seasons" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 437 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I. WINTER IN NORTHUMBERLAND OUTSIDE the garden...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
